prompt:
Platform tooling ticket. Every team edits Terraform Enterprise workspace variables by hand in the UI and we've had two outages from a fat-fingered region value, so workspace vars are going into git and a reconciler pushes them. The repo already has tfe_client.py — a small working /api/v2 transport (bearer auth, JSON:API content type, returns status + parsed body instead of raising on non-2xx). That part is in production; its behavior must not change. What's missing is the reconciler itself.

Do your research before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Terraform Enterprise API documentation on developer.hashicorp.com — the API overview page (auth, media type, JSON:API error documents, the 404 semantics) and the Workspace Variables API reference. Heads up: our old runbook claims the vars list endpoint paginates with page[number]/page[size]; when I checked the current official page it documents no query parameters at all for that list. Verify that against the live docs yourself and call out what you find. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json, provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected fixtures. If the live official docs contradict them, report the conflict explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

The feature — a new module vars_reconciler.py:

- fetch_workspace_vars(client, workspace_id) -> list of dicts {id, key, value, description, category, hcl, sensitive} from the documented list endpoint, in server order. Sensitive variables come back with value null — the API never shows a secret again after it's written; keep that None.
- TFEAPIError — raised for any non-2xx response the reconciler makes; carries .status (int), .errors (the parsed JSON:API errors array), .completed (list of operation strings that already succeeded, empty where not applicable), and str(err) must include the error detail. Don't paper over TFE's 404s: they mean 'missing OR not authorized' and the detail wording must survive.
- plan_changes(current, desired) -> {"create": [...], "update": [...], "delete": [...]}. Identity is the (category, key) pair — the same key in terraform and env is two different variables. Desired entries may omit value/description/hcl/sensitive; fill the documented defaults. A non-sensitive variable needs an update when value, description, or hcl differ. For a sensitive current variable you CANNOT read the value, so never compare it: matching key/category/hcl/description metadata means in sync; changed metadata (or desired sensitive false) means rewrite with the full desired attributes. Turning a plain variable sensitive is an update. Anything in current but not desired is a delete. Every list sorted by (category, key). Update entries carry the current variable's id; deletes carry id/key/category.
- apply_plan(client, workspace_id, plan) -> report list of strings like 'create env/DATADOG_SITE', 'update terraform/region', 'delete env/RETIRED_FLAG'. Execute all creates, then updates, then deletes, in the sorted order. Use the documented request shapes: create POSTs a data.type vars document with the full attribute set; update PATCHes the per-variable path and repeats the id inside data.id; delete sends no body and expects the documented success status. First API error stops the apply and raises TFEAPIError with .completed listing what already ran.
- reconcile(client, workspace_id, desired, dry_run=False) -> {"plan": ..., "report": ...} — fetch, plan, and (unless dry_run) apply. A dry run must not issue a single write request.

Acceptance is test_workspace_vars.py — it runs a loopback fake TFE, no real credentials. Run: python3 test_workspace_vars.py — until it prints all tests passed. test_workspace_vars.py and everything under docs/ are protected; tfe_client.py stays as is.

research.interface: Terraform Enterprise REST API v2 (/api/v2, JSON:API): workspace variables endpoints (stable)

official_sources.title: API documentation for Terraform Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: /api/v2 base path, Authorization: Bearer token auth, application/vnd.api+json JSON:API media type, JSON:API errors array shape, and 404 responses masking unauthorized resources

official_sources.title: /workspaces/{id}/vars API reference for Terraform Enterprise (Workspace Variables)
official_sources.used_for: GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/vars list with no documented pagination parameters, POST create body (data.type vars; key/value/description/category/hcl/sensitive, category terraform or env, defaults), PATCH /workspaces/:workspace_id/vars/:variable_id repeating data.id in the body, DELETE /workspaces/:workspace_id/vars/:variable_id, and the sensitive write-once-not-visible-thereafter rule

research.language_rationale: There is no official HashiCorp Python SDK for the TFE API; the documented integration surface is the raw JSON:API REST contract at /api/v2, and Python's stdlib urllib/json stack is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to implement a small reconciler against it.
